summary: Frontend-only test of MoodifyAI 'Editorial AI Studio' UI/UX. Full e2e flow validated at 1920x1080 and mobile 375x667: idle hero → analyzing → results → analytics → return. All data-testid hooks (mood-input-textarea, mood-input-submit, voice-mic-btn, return-button, analytics-toggle, analytics-back, track-card-*) work. Backend /api/analyze, /api/history, /api/analytics all return valid data. No page errors. Only minor console warnings (React fetchPriority casing & THREE.js rgba alpha).

issues: React console warning: 'React does not recognize the fetchPriority prop on a DOM element. ...spell it as lowercase fetchpriority instead.' Origin: /app/frontend/src/components/TrackCard.jsx:61. Non-blocking but noisy.

THREE.Color warning: 'Alpha component of rgba(...) will be ignored.' Some color values are being passed as rgba() strings to THREE.Color which only accepts rgb. Should split color + opacity or convert to hex. Repeats on every mood change.

GPU stall warnings due to ReadPixels (auto-throttled after first occurrence). Indicates a synchronous gl.readPixels somewhere in the orb/particles pipeline; consider async readback if performance ever degrades. Non-blocking.

action_items: Fix React fetchPriority warning in /app/frontend/src/components/TrackCard.jsx:61 — change attribute to lowercase 'fetchpriority' or remove if not needed.
Clean up THREE.Color rgba(...) warnings in AiOrb/MoodBackground — convert rgba() color strings into separate color + alpha or to hex before passing to THREE.Color.
(Optional) Investigate ReadPixels usage causing GPU stall warnings in the WebGL pipeline.
critical_code_review_comments: TrackCard.jsx passes fetchPriority (camelCase) directly to a native — React DOM expects lowercase 'fetchpriority' attribute name; results in console warning per card.
Color values passed to THREE.Color must not be in rgba() form (alpha ignored); split alpha out cleanly to avoid log noise and ensure deterministic rendering.
verified_features: Idle hero: eyebrow 'AI Emotional Intelligence · v1.0', headline 'Soundtrack your inner world' with italic 'inner', subtitle paragraph, input shell with placeholder + char counter (0/280), voice mic, Synthesize button — all rendered (screenshot 05_idle).
Submit via Enter key AND via Synthesize button click both transition to analyzing then results.
Analyzing phase renders loader (transition completes within ~2s and lands on results).

Results: 'PRIMARY STATE DETECTED' kicker, large title 'Soft Stillness · Evening' (with italic 'Evening'), pills (Intensity/Nuance/Undercurrent/Hidden/Confidence), explanation paragraph, MoodSpectrum (Calmness 28.4%, Introspection 22.6%, etc.) and dimension chips (Energy/Introspection/Depth/Warmth) all rendered.

Right column: 'CURATED FOR YOU' kicker, 'Recommended soundscape' title (italic 'soundscape'), language tabs (English/Hindi/Kannada/Tamil/Telugu), 14 track cards with album art, title, artist, language pill (data-testid=track-card-*).
Top-right time/date widget renders ('11:02 AM / MON, JUN 29').
Top-left Moodify AI logo + 'EMOTIONAL ENGINE' tagline renders.
Bottom-right analytics-toggle navigates to analytics view.

Analytics view: 'ANALYTICS · SESSION HISTORY' kicker, 'Emotional trajectory' title with italic 'trajectory', Intensity Timeline chart (renders without errors despite sparse data — only one bar), Total Logs (20), Most Frequent (Sad), Recent Records list with color dots, italic text snippets and dates.
data-testid=return-button visible in results & analytics, returns to idle and preserves typed text.
ESC key in results returns to idle and preserves text input.
Mobile (375x667): hero, input, layout adapts correctly.
Backend POST /api/analyze (200, full mood payload), GET /api/history (200), GET /api/analytics (200) — all healthy.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All happy paths verified. If retesting, focus on the three warnings flagged (TrackCard fetchPriority, THREE.Color rgba, WebGL ReadPixels) and confirm console is clean after fixes. Status banner (data-testid=status-banner) could not be exercised because no error path triggered it — could be force-tested by temporarily breaking VITE_API_URL.
rca of the issue: fetchPriority warning RCA: React 18 still treats fetchPriority as an unknown DOM attribute on ; React 19 added support. Lowercase 'fetchpriority' or omitting the attribute resolves it. THREE.Color rgba RCA: passing rgba(r,g,b,a) to new THREE.Color() — Three.js parses CSS colors but discards alpha; alpha must be applied via material.opacity / transparent=true.
